summary: Backend testing of 5 new feature families (search, reviews, notifications, messages, earnings + 10% platform fee). 21/22 checks passed. Only 1 real bug: creating a comment on someone else's video does NOT generate a notification (missing create_notification call in POST /videos/{video_id}/comments).
passed: GET /api/search returns {users, videos}; finds users by username
POST /api/services + POST /api/orders work; order creation generates type=order notification for creator
POST /api/reviews rejects unpaid order (400) and rating out of range (400)
GET /api/notifications returns {items, unseen}; POST /api/notifications/mark-seen resets unseen to 0
POST /api/messages/with/{username} creates message and generates type=message notification for receiver
GET /api/messages/with/{username} returns conversation history sorted ASC
GET /api/messages/conversations returns list with last_text and unread per conversation
GET /api/earnings/me returns {total_gross, total_fees, total_earned, orders_count, platform_fee_percent=10.0}

POST /api/payments/checkout creates real Stripe session; GET /api/payments/status/{sid} returns clean structure and does NOT mark order paid without actual Stripe payment (payment_status stays 'unpaid')

PLATFORM_FEE_PERCENT=10.0 constant present; fee branch logic verified in payment_status and webhook code paths (lines 511, 522, 546, 556 in server.py) — stores platform_fee & creator_earnings on both payment_transactions and orders on successful payment

failed.issue: Creating a comment on another user's video does NOT create a notification of type='comment' for the video owner. The POST /api/videos/{video_id}/comments endpoint (server.py lines 348-361) never calls create_notification().
failed.evidence: Test uploaded video by u1, u2 posted comment successfully (200), then GET /api/notifications for u1 returned only ['order'] — no 'comment' entry. Expected per review request: 'Creating a comment on someone else's video generates a notification for the video owner (type=comment)'.

action_items: In /app/backend/server.py add_comment() (~line 348-361): after inserting comment, look up the video owner and call create_notification(video_owner_id, 'comment', f'@{user["username"]} علّق على فيديوك', video_id, user['id']) — but only if user['id'] != video owner id.
notes: Could not fully validate the 10% fee is written to payment_transactions & orders end-to-end because that requires an actual Stripe test-card completion (not scriptable via API). However, code inspection confirms the branch logic is correct in BOTH payment_status polling and webhook handlers. All other new endpoints verified live against the public URL.
